summary: Started the Pikachu fighter decompilation by moving a large first block of ftpikachu.s text into new C files under src/melee/ft/chara/ftPikachu, adding a Pikachu attribute header, wiring the new objects into obj_files.mk, and leaving remaining data/asm in place. The PR also corrected the Fighter ftcmd variable field names by offset, then propagated those names through existing Mars, Master Hand, Young Link, and Dr. Mario code. There were no review comments in the slice; the author described it as an early start that did not conflict with other work.
what_changed: Removed roughly 800 lines of Pikachu text assembly from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpikachu.s and replaced it with new C translation units ftpikachu1.c, ftpikachu2.c, and ftpikachu3.c.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftPikachu/ftpikachu.h with an ftPikachuAttributes struct covering known offsets from x0 through xF4, including explicit padding before xDC.

Implemented early Pikachu lifecycle and helper functions in C, including func_801243AC, ftPikachu_OnLoad, ftPikachu_OnDeath, item visibility helpers, special-attribute copy/scale handling, and simple callbacks.

Implemented Pikachu action-state entry and per-frame routines for action states 0x155, 0x156, and 0x157, including ftcmd var resets, projectile spawn positioning through func_802B338C, sound calls, ground/air transitions, accessory effects, and callback setup.
Added the three Pikachu C object files to obj_files.mk before the remaining ftpikachu.s object so the decompiled text could replace corresponding assembly while preserving undecompiled code/data.
Updated Pichu assembly action callback table entries from lbl_80124908-style local labels to func_80124908-style symbol names, matching the newly emitted C function symbols.

Renamed Fighter struct fields at offsets 0x2204, 0x2208, and 0x220C from x2200_ftcmd_var1/2/3 to x2204_ftcmd_var1, x2208_ftcmd_var2, and x220C_ftcmd_var3, then fixed existing callsites in Mars, Master Hand, Young Link, and Dr. Mario to use offset-correct names.
Removed src/m2ctx.py from the tree and added src/m2ctx.py plus ctx.c to .gitignore under an 'Other' section.
smart_moves: Split the initial Pikachu port into numbered files matching the existing assembly chunking, which made a large decomp start easier to wire in incrementally while keeping ftpikachu.s for the remaining undecompiled code.
Kept remaining assembly/data linked by adding C objects before asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpikachu.s.o in obj_files.mk rather than trying to convert the whole character at once.

Used full-struct assignment for ftPikachuAttributes copies from ftData->ext_attr into fighter special-attribute storage, reflecting the source assembly's repeated block-copy pattern and reducing manual field-copy risk.
Corrected shared Fighter ftcmd variable names by actual byte offset, preventing misleading x2200_* names for fields that live at 0x2204, 0x2208, and 0x220C.
Propagated the ftcmd var rename across unrelated fighters in the same PR, avoiding a partially-renamed core struct that would make later decompilation harder to search and reason about.

Left explicit TODO/delete-me comments for unfinished header prototypes and temporary extern float labels in ftpikachu3.c, making incomplete data migration visible rather than pretending the file was finished.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling a fighter in slices, object ordering can let new C replace a leading range of assembly while undecompiled assembly continues to provide the rest of the file.
Core struct field names should encode the real offset when the semantic name is still generic; x2204_ftcmd_var1 is more searchable and less misleading than x2200_ftcmd_var1.
Action command variables are shared infrastructure across many fighters, so renaming them in Fighter requires sweeping all current users, not just the newly decompiled character.
A character-specific attributes struct can start with offset-based fields and padding as long as it preserves layout; semantic field names can come later when behavior is better understood.
Block-copy assembly patterns around fighter special attributes can often become C struct assignments, but the struct size and padding must match the copied range.
Early decomp PRs may legitimately contain unnamed func_ symbols, temporary extern data labels, and TODO headers; those markers are useful follow-up anchors if clearly scoped.
naming_conventions: Character source directory used camel-cased ftPikachu with lowercase file names: src/melee/ft/chara/ftPikachu/ftpikachu1.c, ftpikachu2.c, ftpikachu3.c, and ftpikachu.h.
Character attribute type introduced as ftPikachuAttributes with offset-style members x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14, x18, x1C, etc.
Lifecycle functions kept established character naming where known: ftPikachu_OnLoad and ftPikachu_OnDeath.
Unknown functions remained address-derived, e.g. func_801243AC, func_80124830, func_80124DC8, and func_80124E38.
Fighter ftcmd variables were renamed to include their actual struct offsets: x2200_ftcmd_var0, x2204_ftcmd_var1, x2208_ftcmd_var2, and x220C_ftcmd_var3.
Assembly table references should use function symbol names when C provides the symbol, as shown by Pichu entries changing from lbl_80124908 to func_80124908.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted loops that copy Pikachu extended attributes into direct C assignments such as *pika_attr = *ext_attr.
Preserved stack-shaping hints with unused local arrays such as s32 unused[2] and stack_buffer[2] in functions where matching likely depended on stack layout.
Used temporary variables like tempObj/tempObj2 in accessory effect routines, likely to influence register allocation or evaluation order around ef_Spawn calls.
Kept float constants as temporary extern labels lbl_804D9910 and lbl_804D9914 in ftpikachu3.c until the data side could be moved over.

Maintained C calls matching known engine routines such as Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, func_8006EBA4, func_80070FB4, func_80074A4C, func_8026B3F8, and func_802B338C rather than abstracting behavior prematurely.
Updated external assembly callback tables to point at the new C function symbols so cross-file references continued to resolve after removing text from ftpikachu.s.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish or rename functions in src/melee/ft/chara/ftPikachu/ftpikachu1.c through ftpikachu3.c.
Find later changes replacing temporary extern float labels lbl_804D9910 and lbl_804D9914 with proper data declarations.
Search for ftPikachuAttributes semantic renames after this offset-only struct introduction.
Check whether the TODO function prototypes in ftpikachu.h were later completed or moved to a shared character header.
Search for additional uses or corrections of x2204_ftcmd_var1, x2208_ftcmd_var2, and x220C_ftcmd_var3 after this core Fighter rename.
